step: Read and analyze backend pom.xml dependencies and boilerplate structure
sub_steps: Open and review /home/coder/project/workspace/question_generation_service/solutions/a58cad67-f13a-415f-9eb9-069550f67951/springapp/pom.xml to understand provided dependencies (Spring Boot, JPA, MySQL, Validation).
Note project structure, ensure MySQL is used, and confirm expected packages for entities, repositories, services, controllers.
Check RealEstateListingManagementSystemApplication.java for base package information.
description: This step is crucial to understand what libraries are available and to verify the foundational setup for implementing the Spring Boot backend according to requirements.

sub_steps: Create Property.java under the correct package (com.examly.springapp.model or equivalent).
Define fields with appropriate types, add JPA and validation annotations (e.g., @Id, @GeneratedValue, @Column, @NotNull, @Size, @Positive, etc.).
Implement field-level constraints for required fields and field lengths.
Use @Entity and @Table annotations as necessary.
description: Defines the main data model for properties, ensures all validation and JPA mapping requirements are met per the specification. This sets the foundation for all subsequent layers.

sub_steps: Create PropertyRepository.java interface in the repository package (com.examly.springapp.repository or equivalent).
Extend JpaRepository<Property, Long>.
Define custom query methods for filtering by price, bedrooms, city using Spring Data JPA method naming conventions or @Query annotations as needed.
description: Allows data access for Property entities, providing CRUD and filter/search capabilities as required by business logic and endpoints.

sub_steps: Create PropertyService.java and PropertyServiceImpl.java in service package.
Define methods to add new property, get all properties, get property by ID, and filter properties by criteria.
Inject PropertyRepository and handle business logic, including throwing exceptions for not found, handling validation, etc.
description: Implements business logic required for all property-related operations, ensuring correct delegation to the data access layer and applying business rules (e.g., validation, not found handling).

step: Implement PropertyController with REST endpoints and exception handling
sub_steps: Create PropertyController.java in controller package.
Define REST endpoints: POST /api/properties, GET /api/properties, GET /api/properties/{id}, GET /api/properties/filter.
Integrate validation (using @Valid), proper status codes (201, 200, 400, 404), and return appropriate error messages.
Handle field validation errors and resource not found exceptions via @ExceptionHandler or @ControllerAdvice as needed.
Implement CORS configuration to allow access from the React frontend.
description: Exposes the backend functionality as RESTful endpoints, enforcing validation, business logic, status codes, and error handling conforming to the question requirements and enabling frontend integration.

step: Implement ALL Backend Test Cases with JUnit for Property functionality
sub_steps: For createPropertyTest: Add test methods to check property creation, validation errors (missing fields, invalid values).
For getPropertiesTest: Add test methods to check retrieving all properties, including empty list scenario.
For getPropertyByIdTest: Add tests for retrieving by valid and invalid (non-existent) ID.
For filterPropertiesTest: Add tests that cover the filtering endpoint for various scenarios (price range, bedrooms, city, combined, and empty results).
Use @SpringBootTest or WebMvcTest with Mockito for service/repository mocking as needed.
Add or edit PropertyControllerTest.java or similarly named test in the test directory.
description: Fully implements the backend testing requirements as defined in the test cases, covering both positive and negative scenarios according to question and JSON test mapping.

step: Compile and run backend (Spring Boot) - Build and Test
sub_steps: In /home/coder/project/workspace/question_generation_service/solutions/a58cad67-f13a-415f-9eb9-069550f67951/springapp, run: mvn clean compile -q -Dspring.main.banner-mode=off
Then run: mvn test -q -Dspring.main.banner-mode=off
Then run: mvn clean install -q -Dspring.main.banner-mode=off
description: Ensures the backend is correctly compiled, all tests are passing, and the project is ready for integration with the frontend.

step: Read and analyze frontend package.json dependencies and boilerplate structure
sub_steps: Open and review /home/coder/project/workspace/question_generation_service/solutions/a58cad67-f13a-415f-9eb9-069550f67951/reactapp/package.json and note dependencies (React, axios, testing-library/react, etc.).
Check existing files under src/ for structure and entry points.
Plan component and utility file locations according to the observed structure.
description: Lays the foundation for implementation by understanding the frontend project's module setup and dependencies, which affect API integration and component patterns.

sub_steps: Create PropertyList.js in components/.
Implement a component to display a list of property cards with title, price (formatted), bedrooms, bathrooms, area, and city.
Add click handler to select property for detail view (lift state to parent or via props).
Style grid/list per UI requirements with consistent design.
Immediately create PropertyList.test.js for all related test cases: rendering, API mocking, formatting, interaction.
description: Implements the main property list view with the required data, user interactions, and styling as needed for both display and test case compliance.

sub_steps: Create PropertyDetail.js in components/.
Display complete details for the selected property: title, description, price, bedrooms, bathrooms, area, address, city, state, zipCode, propertyType, listingDate, availability.
Add a "Back to List" button/option and wire up callback.
Style the component in accordance with UI requirements (panel/card layout, label/value pairs).
Create PropertyDetail.test.js covering rendering of all attributes for valid property, click of Back button.
description: Provides detailed view for a property and supports user navigation, covering requirements for scenario-based and attribute rendering in tests.

sub_steps: Create PropertyFilter.js in components/.
Add inputs for minPrice, maxPrice, bedrooms, city; bind to state.
Add 'Filter' button to trigger API calls to filter endpoint; a 'Clear' button resets all inputs.
Handle loading and error scenarios on filter API calls.
Create PropertyFilter.test.js to mock API and check interactions, input clear, and resulting list updates.
description: Implements the property filtering feature and all associated tests, providing state management for input fields and seamless integration with the listing, covering all filter/test requirements.

sub_steps: Create api.js in utils/.
Implement fetchAllProperties(), fetchPropertyById(id), filterProperties(criteria), each using axios or fetch with correct API URLs and error handling.
Export functions for use in components.
Ensure error responses are handled and propagated for UI display.
description: Centralizes all API calls, abstracts error handling, and allows for simple mocking in tests. Enables consistent API usage across components.

step: Integrate components in App.js with state management and UI composition
sub_steps: Edit App.js to import PropertyList, PropertyDetail, and PropertyFilter; manage global state as needed for properties, selected property, and filter criteria.
Wire up data flow and handlers (select/deselect property, apply/clear filters, etc.)
Display loading indicators, error messages, and empty states as required.
Apply overall CSS layout and ensure responsive design.
Reference and update App.css as needed for consistent application-wide styling and CSS variables.
description: Brings together all components, manages shared state, and provides the main user interface including loading/error/empty handling, as well as the glue logic for navigation between list/detail and applying filters.

step: Implement ALL Frontend Test Cases with React Testing Library/Jest
sub_steps: For renderPropertyListTest: In PropertyList.test.js, mock API call for all properties, verify render, correct cards, title/price/fields, price formatting.
For propertyFilteringTest: In PropertyFilter.test.js, render filter form, simulate input/clear, test filter button, mock API, check filtered results.
For propertyDetailViewTest: In PropertyDetail.test.js, simulate click from PropertyList, check all property attributes, test Back button navigation.
For apiErrorHandlingTest: In App.test.js or relevant component test, mock API failures, display error, check loading indicator, test application resilience.
Use async patterns, waitFor, proper data-testid selectors, and API mocks matching test case descriptions.
No manual cleanup() calls; ensure fresh renders per scenario as per guidelines.
description: Ensures every frontend functionality is tested as prescribed—list rendering, filtering, detail view, and error/loading handling—with proper isolation, no ambiguous selectors, and strict mapping to test names and requirements.

step: Compile and run frontend (React) - Build, Lint, and Test
sub_steps: In /home/coder/project/workspace/question_generation_service/solutions/a58cad67-f13a-415f-9eb9-069550f67951/reactapp, run: npm install
Then run: npm run build
Then run: npx eslint src/ --fix
Then run: npm run test:ci
description: Verifies that the React app builds successfully, is lint-free, and passes all supplied Jest/RTL tests, confirming readiness for deployment and functional correctness.
